one_line: Legendary master of statecraft and authorial name behind the Arthashastra’s science of governance.

appearance.age_look: Mid-40s, lean and ascetic build with wiry muscle, standing straight with a commanding but restrained presence.
appearance.face: Long oval face with sharp nose, high cheekbones, medium-brown complexion, slightly sunken cheeks, thin lips, and narrow intense eyes; a small vertical tilak of pale sandalwood on the forehead; default expression is calm, calculating focus with a hint of sternness.
appearance.hair: Fully shaven head except for a single long shikha at the back, about mid-back length, tied into a neat dark-brown/black braid; no headwear.
appearance.costume: Simple yet authoritative Brahmin/ministerial attire: a plain unstitched dhoti in off-white undyed cotton, wrapped to mid-calf with neat pleats in front; an upper cloth (uttariya) of fine saffron-ochre cotton draped over the left shoulder and under the right arm in a scholar’s style, the end hanging to mid-thigh; bare upper torso except for the uttariya, showing a single sacred thread (yajnopavita) across his chest; simple brown leather sandals; minimal jewellery limited to a single thin copper bangle on the right wrist and a small wooden bead bracelet on the left; a narrow cloth belt in muted maroon securing a small cloth pouch at his waist, suggesting access to documents and seals.
appearance.signature: A rolled palm-leaf manuscript bundle bound with a dark cord, tucked under his left arm and partially unfurled in his right hand, its wooden cover edges visible, symbolizing the Arthashastra as a living manual of governance rather than a battlefield weapon.

ref_desc: Kautilya is a mid-40s lean, medium-brown man with an oval face, intense narrow eyes, a long braided shikha, off-white dhoti, saffron-ochre uttariya, sacred thread, simple sandals, a copper bangle, and a rolled palm-leaf Arthashastra manuscript bundle bound with dark cord.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style character sheet of Kautilya as a historically grounded Mauryan-era Brahmin statesman: a mid-40s lean, medium-brown man with a long oval face, sharp nose, high cheekbones, narrow intense eyes, thin lips, and a small vertical pale sandalwood tilak on his forehead, his expression calm and calculating. His head is completely shaved except for a single long dark brown-black shikha at the back, braided neatly to mid-back length. He wears an off-white undyed cotton dhoti wrapped to mid-calf with precise front pleats, and a fine saffron-ochre cotton uttariya draped over his left shoulder and under his right arm so his upper torso is mostly bare, a sacred thread running diagonally across his chest. Add a simple narrow cloth belt in muted maroon securing a small cloth pouch at his waist, a thin copper bangle on his right wrist, a small wooden bead bracelet on his left, and plain brown leather sandals on his feet; no other jewellery or headwear. His signature prop is a rolled palm-leaf manuscript bundle with visible wooden cover edges, bound tightly with a dark cord, tucked under his left arm while his right hand partially unfurls it, emphasizing him as the authorial mind of the Arthashastra rather than a warrior. Use a restrained palette of off-white, saffron-ochre, muted maroon, medium-brown skin tones, and dark brown-black hair, and present front, side, and back views in the same outfit to emphasize a consistent design.
